_doc: Worked example route-table for modelpipe (src/router.mjs). Copy it, set the env vars it names, then start the router and point your Anthropic-format client at it: `modelpipe routes.json` then `export ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8787`. The router keys on each request body's `model` and forwards to the FIRST route whose `match` glob matches (so order specific routes before broad ones). Keys are referenced by env-var NAME only — NEVER put a key value in this file. Provider endpoints/auth come from providers.json (verified 2026-06-30).

_route_shape.match: REQUIRED string — glob over the model id; only `*` is a wildcard (e.g. claude-* / deepseek-* / google/*)
_route_shape.base_url: REQUIRED string — the backend origin (optionally with a base path, e.g. /anthropic); the client's /v1/messages path is appended
_route_shape.auth: REQUIRED — EITHER the string "passthrough" (forward the client's own auth header unchanged — for a subscription/OAuth session with no backend key) OR an object { header, keyEnv, scheme?, fallback? }: the header name to send the backend key under, the NAME of the env var holding the key (read at request time, never logged), an OPTIONAL scheme prefix ("Bearer" yields `Authorization: Bearer <key>`; omit for a raw value like `x-api-key: <key>`), and OPTIONAL fallback:true (forward the client's OWN token when it sent one, inject keyEnv only when it didn't — 'the token that flies wins'). NOTE: a subscription OAuth token (sk-ant-oat…) is short-lived/auto-refreshed — prefer "passthrough" for it; use fallback with a long-lived API key.
_route_shape.billing: OPTIONAL "metered" | "subscription". How the dashboard reports money for this backend. "metered" = pay-as-you-go, real per-token $ shown (DeepSeek, OpenRouter). "subscription" = a flat plan (GLM Coding Plan, an Anthropic subscription) where per-token $ is meaningless — the dashboard shows tokens + a "flat plan" label, never a fabricated cost. DEFAULT: passthrough ⇒ subscription, key-swap ⇒ metered.
_route_shape.accounts: OPTIONAL array of multiple accounts/keys for the SAME model — the router rotates to the next when one hits its limit (429/529/quota/credit). Each is { label, auth, base_url? }: a label (its own dashboard card + per-label stats), its own auth (same shape as route auth), and an optional base_url override. When present, route-level auth is OPTIONAL. Key/backend-level rotation — the model id is NOT rewritten. Cooldown recovery via failoverRecoveryIntervalMs; GET /v1/accounts + POST /v1/accounts/reset for state. Opt-in per route.
_route_shape.strategy: OPTIONAL "failover" (default) | "round-robin" — only with accounts. failover drains account #1 and moves to #2 on a limit (snaps back on recovery); round-robin spreads requests across accounts to stretch total quota.
_route_shape.forImages: OPTIONAL true on exactly ONE route — marks it the vision fallback target. When a backend rejects an image-bearing request with an image-unsupported 400, modelpipe reroutes the request to this route.
_route_shape.forImagesModel: REQUIRED on the forImages route — the model id the vision backend expects (e.g. an OpenRouter vendor/model id like google/gemini-2.5-flash-lite). On reroute, ONLY the body's `model` is rewritten to this; the rest of the body (the image bytes) is untouched. Fail-closed: a forImages route with no forImagesModel is a config error at startup.
_route_shape.vision: OPTIONAL boolean, default true. false ⇒ this route's backend has no vision capability. An image-bearing request matched by this route goes STRAIGHT to the forImages target (with the forImagesModel rewrite), never sent to this backend first. Use it for a backend that does NOT 400 on an image (a 200 soft-refusal, or its own server-side image tool) — the reactive catch-400 fallback cannot detect those. Text-only turns route normally. Inert with no forImages route configured.

_config_shape.dashboard: OPTIONAL boolean, default false — enables the /dashboard monitoring page, /v1/stats, /v1/quotas, /v1/sessions, and per-model usage tracking (zero-buffer, never impacts latency). The live session + dashboard-set overrides persist under ~/.modelpipe/ across restarts.
_config_shape.tokenPrices: OPTIONAL object { modelGlob: { input, output } } — per-model metered API prices in $ per 1M tokens; keys may use * globs; falls back to the built-in catalog. Editable at runtime (⚙ → token prices) and persisted.
_config_shape.failover: OPTIONAL object { modelGlob: backupModelId } — per-model backup on a retryable error (see _failover_doc).
_config_shape.failoverGroups: OPTIONAL array of ordered ladders (see _failoverGroups_doc) — coordinated 'shift the whole ladder' failover.
_config_shape.schedules: OPTIONAL array of time-of-day model rewrites (see _schedules_doc) — proactively route away from a peak-priced model during set hours.
_config_shape.maxBodyBytes: OPTIONAL number, default 26214400 (25 MB) — request body size cap; requests exceeding it get a 413
_config_shape.proxyUrl: OPTIONAL string — surfaced in `modelpipe --list` output for a client setup dialog
_config_shape.concurrency: OPTIONAL object { modelGlob: maxConcurrent } — max SIMULTANEOUS in-flight requests per (provider, model). Some providers cap concurrent requests per subscription/key (e.g. z.ai GLM Coding Plan allows only a few glm-5.2 at once); firing more just earns a 429 that failover then 'fixes' by degrading to a weaker model. Instead, the overflow is held in a FIFO QUEUE until a slot frees, so the client keeps the strong model — it just waits. FIRST MATCH WINS (order specific ids before broad globs: glm-5.2 before glm-*). Unmatched = unlimited (zero overhead). PER ACCOUNT/KEY: keyed by (providerId, model) and providerId is the account label for a pool, so a 2-account pool carries 2x the limit. A request that can't get a slot within concurrencyQueueTimeoutMs is treated as a backend 429 (→ account rotation → model failover: 'wait, then failover'). Editable at runtime (GET/POST /v1/concurrency) and persisted. See docs/failover.md#concurrency-limiting.
_config_shape.concurrencyQueueTimeoutMs: OPTIONAL number, default 45000. How long a request may wait in a full concurrency queue before it is treated as a backend 429 (→ account rotation / model failover). Must be >= 1000. Keep it under your client's request timeout so the safety valve fires first.
_config_shape.compact: OPTIONAL object — context FITTING (a safety net, not steady-state compaction), ON BY DEFAULT (see docs/compaction.md). Handles the failover downshift: when a request running against a big-window model fails over to a smaller-window backup, the grown conversation no longer fits — the client can't prevent this (only the proxy knows it rerouted), so on a hop to a smaller-window model the request is mechanically trimmed to fit, dropping older turns to a stable checkpoint with tool_use/tool_result pairs intact (never a dangling-pair 400). If a backend still rejects a request as too long, the real window is learned (from the error message, else ~90% of what was sent) and persisted per model, then the request is hard-trimmed and retried. No summarizer, zero added latency on the normal path; steady-state compaction is left to the harness (Claude Code's native auto-compact). Fields: { enabled(bool), safetyPct(0,1] trim to this fraction of the window, windowDefault(tokens for unmatched models), window({modelGlob:tokens} — FIRST MATCH WINS, order specific ids before broad globs), maxOverflowRetries(int>=0) }. Editable at runtime (dashboard ⚙) and persisted; learned windows in ~/.modelpipe/compact-learned-windows.json.

_concurrency_doc: Per-(provider,model) simultaneous-request caps — the overflow is QUEUED, not degraded to a backup (see _config_shape.concurrency and docs/failover.md#concurrency-limiting). FIRST MATCH WINS so the specific tiers (glm-5.2=3, glm-5.1=6) are listed BEFORE the broad glm-*=8 catch-all; deepseek-v4-pro=200 is effectively unlimited (metered pay-as-you-go, no concurrency cap to respect). The limit is PER ACCOUNT/KEY: on a route with an `accounts` pool the key is the account label, so a 2-account pool carries 2x the number here (3 ⇒ up to 6 concurrent across two live keys) and a parked/exhausted key simply contributes 0 — modelpipe scales to whatever keys are live, no need to bake the account count into the number. z.ai's GLM Coding Plan meters concurrency per model per subscription; these keep modelpipe from tripping that and needlessly failing over. Remove this block for no concurrency limiting (unlimited).
_compact_doc: Context fitting (ON by default) — a safety net for failover downshift, see the _config_shape.compact entry above and docs/compaction.md. Trims a request to fit the TARGET model's window when it's routed to a smaller-window backup than the client's model. window maps model ids/globs to their context size, FIRST MATCH WINS so list specific ids before broad globs (glm-5.2=1M before glm-*=200K). Steady-state compaction is the harness's job, not this. Remove this block to keep defaults.
_failover_doc: OPTIONAL object. Maps model globs to backup model ids. When a primary model returns a rate-limit (429), overloaded (529), or temporary-unavailable 5xx error, the router rewrites the body model and reroutes to the backup. Chain depth-guarded at 5 hops. A background pinger probes primaries and clears failover on recovery. NOTE: a model that also rides a failoverGroups ladder is handled by the GROUP, not this pair map.
_failoverGroups_doc: OPTIONAL array of ordered ladders — a shared priority chain multiple models ride together. mode:"shift" (default): when the HEAD tier fails (e.g. Anthropic down), the WHOLE ladder shifts down one — opus→glm AND glm's own traffic→deepseek at the same time — and winds back when the head recovers. mode:"cascade": walk the ladder per-request on error (no coordinated shift). The head (index 0) may be a glob; every lower tier is a rewrite target and MUST be a concrete model id. Groups take precedence over `failover` pairs. Remove this block if you only want per-model pairs.
_failoverRecoveryIntervalMs_doc: OPTIONAL number, default 60000. Minimum ms between recovery pings to a failed-over primary (and between group re-probe/wind-back). Must be >= 1000.
_schedules_doc: OPTIONAL array of time-of-day model rewrites (proactive cost control). While any window is open, a request whose model matches `match` is rewritten to the cheaper `to` BEFORE routing/failover/vision — as if the client had asked for `to`. Each entry: `match` (model glob), `to` (concrete replacement model id — must resolve to a route), `tz` (a FIXED UTC offset like "+08:00" / "-0530" / "Z", NOT a named zone), `windows` (non-empty array of ["HH:MM","HH:MM"] 24h pairs; from>to wraps past midnight; the range is [from,to) — start inclusive, end exclusive). Times are read in `tz` and evaluated against the SYSTEM CLOCK via the UTC epoch, so they're correct no matter how the host's local timezone is set. First matching schedule with an open window wins; `to` == the incoming model is a no-op. Unlike failover (reactive, on error) this fires on EVERY matching request in-window. Editable at runtime (⚙ Settings → Scheduled routing) and persisted. EXAMPLE ABOVE: z.ai bills GLM-5.2 / GLM-5-Turbo at 3x quota during peak (14:00–18:00 UTC+8); off-peak tiers glm-5.1 / glm-4.5-air carry no multiplier, so peak traffic drops to them and stays on the flat Coding Plan. Off-peak it uses the full-strength models normally.

routes._note: Anthropic route: claude-* ids land at api.anthropic.com with the client's own auth (OAuth/subscription). billing:subscription ⇒ the dashboard shows tokens + real rate-limit headers, never a fabricated $ cost. Change auth to { header: x-api-key, keyEnv: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY } (and billing:metered) if using a pay-as-you-go API key instead.

GLM route: e.g. set ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL=glm-5.2 and sonnet calls land here. ZAI_API_KEY is the Coding Plan token from z.ai console. billing:subscription ⇒ the Coding Plan is flat, so the dashboard shows tokens + a link to the z.ai console for the real quota, never a per-token $ estimate.

DeepSeek route: pay-as-you-go, so billing defaults to metered — the dashboard shows real per-response $ from token counts × price, plus the live account balance. vision:false — DeepSeek soft-refuses images with a 200, so image-bearing requests are pre-routed straight to the forImages target without hitting DeepSeek first. DEEPSEEK_API_KEY is from platform.deepseek.com.

Vision fallback: marks this route as the forImages target. When any backend rejects an image, the request is rerouted here with body.model rewritten to forImagesModel. Also catches direct google/* calls.
